item.title: From Moon hotels to cattle herding: 8 startups investors chased at YC Demo Day
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: We polled nearly a dozen VCs to find out which W26 startups are the sought after in the batch.

item.title: Aetherflux reportedly raising Series B at $2 billion valuation
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Index Ventures is said to be leading a $250 million to $350 million round.

item.title: VCs are betting billions on AI’s next wave, so why is OpenAI killing Sora?
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Anthony Ha, Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane
item.description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back.  That tension is everywhere […]

item.title: OpenAI shuts down Sora while Meta gets shut out in court
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back.  That tension is everywhere […]

item.title: 16 of the most interesting startups from YC W26 Demo Day
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: From redirecting doomscrolling to training humanoid robots, these 16 startups stood out as the most interesting ones from an overflowing YC cohort.

item.title: Defense startup Shield AI lands $12.7B valuation, up 140%, after US Air Force deal
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: In one year, Shield AI's value has leaped 140%. This after it won a contract to be the software provider to Anduril's Fury fighter jet for the U.S. Air Force.

item.title: Silicon Valley’s two biggest dramas have intersected: LiteLLM and Delve
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: LiteLLM offers an AI open source project used by millions that was infected by credential harvesting malware.

item.title: Why hiring the weirdos works
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: When you’re building at breakneck speed, hiring a trusted team is crucial for an early-stage startup. Bland CEO and co-founder Isaiah Granet has tactical advice on how the company managed to find hidden talent in unlikely places.

item.title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

item.title: Mercor competitor Deccan AI raises $25M, sources experts from India
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.

item.title: Riding the GLP-1 boom, VITL lands $7.5M to overhaul cash-pay clinic prescribing
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The startup provides an e-prescribing marketplace for the booming cash-pay clinic market.

item.title: How soap opera-TikTok hybrids became a billion-dollar business
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Over the past few years, a new category of mobile apps has quietly exploded into a multi-billion dollar business. They’re called “micro dramas” — short-form, mobile-first scripted shows designed to be watched vertically on your phone. Think soap opera meets TikTok, complete with secret billionaire romances, disapproving werewolf mothers-in-law, and cliffhangers engineered to keep users tapping. The leading […]

item.title: Harvey confirms $11B valuation: Sequoia triples down
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Investors like Sequoia, Andreessen Horowitz, Kleiner Perkins, and Elad Gil can't get enough of AI legal tech startup Harvey.

item.title: ReelShort made $1.2 billion on werewolf romances. Watch Club wants to do it better.
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Rebecca Bellan, Amanda Silberling
item.description: Over the past few years, a new category of mobile apps has quietly exploded into a multi-billion dollar business. They’re called “microdramas” — short-form, mobile-first scripted shows designed to be watched vertically on your phone. Think soap opera meets TikTok, complete with secret billionaire romances, disapproving werewolf mothers-in-law, and cliffhangers engineered to keep users tapping. The leading app, ReelShort, […]

item.title: After pivoting, Y Combinator grad Glimpse raises $35M led by a16z
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Glimpse announced on Wednesday a $35 million Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from 8VC and YC.

item.title: Harbinger’s next product will be hybrid emergency vehicles
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The young California startup is already building RVs, FedEx vans, and box trucks. Now its hybrid powertrain will underpin ambulances and mobile healthcare units.

item.title: Arinna raises $4M seed round to solve the space power problem
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Arinna builds solar cells specifically for spacecraft and has developed an ultrathin new material that it expects to be far more flexible and more efficient than current technology.

item.title: Lucid Bots raises $20M to keep up with demand for its window-washing drones
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Lucid Bots has seen demand accelerate over the last year for its window-cleaning drones and power-washing robots.
